info.name: RBAC – User Permissions (/admin/users/{user_id}/permissions)
info.description:
E2E tests for the user direct-permission grant API.

Routes covered:
  GET    /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/permissions
  POST   /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/permissions
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/permissions/{grant_id}

Folder execution order:
  1. setup            — register + Gmail OTP + verify-email + login
                        (captures up_access_token — plain user, no RBAC permissions yet)
                        also registers a second target user whose grants are managed
  2. auth-guard       — 401 on all three routes with no Authorization header
  3. rbac-guard       — 403 on all three routes with valid JWT but no permissions
  4. owner-bootstrap  — PUT /rbac/owner/assign promotes the acting user to owner
                        (owner role grants rbac:read + rbac:grant_user_perm; RBAC middleware
                        reads DB per request — no re-login needed)
  5. happy-path       — full lifecycle: GET (empty) → capture perm → POST grant → GET (one
                        entry; capture up_grant_id) → DELETE revoke → GET (empty again)
  6. not-found        — DELETE unknown grant → 404 grant_not_found
                        POST to invalid (non-UUID) user_id → 404 user_not_found
                        POST with valid role but unknown permission_id → 422 permission_not_found
  7. validation       — 422/400/413 input-error cases for POST
  8. conflict         — 409 permission_already_granted when grant already exists

Guard order (handler):
  GET    /rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions             : JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:read)            → handler
  POST   /rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions             : JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:grant_user_perm) → handler
  DELETE /rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions/{grant_id}  : JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:grant_user_perm) → handler

The admin router applies AllowContentType("application/json") before JWTAuth, so POST
requests in auth-guard must carry Content-Type: application/json.

Environment variables required:
  bootstrap_secret   — must match BOOTSTRAP_SECRET in .env
  gmail_test_email   — Gmail address for OTP receipt
  gmail_access_token — OAuth2 token for Gmail API reads

Notes on what is NOT covered here (covered by unit tests):
  - DB / store errors → 500                          — handler_test.go, service_test.go
  - ErrPrivilegeEscalation → 403                    — store_test.go (owner holds all perms;
                                                       not testable via black-box e2e)
  - ErrScopeNotAllowed → 422 scope_not_allowed       — store_test.go (depends on seed
                                                       scope_policy; not black-box verifiable)
  - UUID parse failure in service (bad user_id)      — service_test.go (covered separately)
  - nil permissions marshals as [] not null           — handler_test.go

Run `make _e2e-db-clean` and `make _e2e-kv-clean` before each suite run.
Requires TRUSTED_PROXIES=127.0.0.1/32 in .env.

exec: if (!email) { console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.'); }
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API (async)...');
pm.sendRequest({ url: 'https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/messages?q=' + query + '&maxResults=3', method: 'GET', header: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + token } }, (err, res) => {
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found.'); return; }
pm.sendRequest({ url: 'https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/messages/' + msgs[0].id + '?format=full', method: 'GET', header: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + token } }, (err2, res2) => {
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }

if (parts && parts.length) { const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html'); if (p && p.body && p.body.data) body = atob(p.body.data.replace(/-/g, '+').replace(/_/g, '/')); }
else if (msg.payload && msg.payload.body && msg.payload.body.data) { body = atob(msg.payload.body.data.replace(/-/g, '+').replace(/_/g, '/')); }
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] OTP stored in collection variable "up_acting_code":', match[1]);
const code = pm.collectionVariables.get('up_acting_code'); if (!code) { console.error('[ERROR] up_acting_code is empty.'); }
console.log('[INFO] Captured up_access_token and up_acting_user_id:', payload.sub);
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch for target user'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for target user verification email...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API for target user...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + targetEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
pm.sendRequest({ url: 'https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/messages?q=' + query + '&maxResults=3', method: 'GET', header: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + token } }, (err, res) => {
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found for target user.'); return; }
pm.sendRequest({ url: 'https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/messages/' + msgs[0].id + '?format=full', method: 'GET', header: { Authorization: 'Bearer ' + token } }, (err2, res2) => {
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }

if (parts && parts.length) { const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html'); if (p && p.body && p.body.data) body = atob(p.body.data.replace(/-/g, '+').replace(/_/g, '/')); }
else if (msg.payload && msg.payload.body && msg.payload.body.data) { body = atob(msg.payload.body.data.replace(/-/g, '+').replace(/_/g, '/')); }
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode target email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] OTP stored in collection variable "up_target_code":', match[1]);
const code = pm.collectionVariables.get('up_target_code'); if (!code) { console.error('[ERROR] up_target_code is empty.'); }
if (id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('up_target_user_id', id); console.log('[INFO] Captured up_target_user_id:', id); } else { console.warn('[WARN] Could not extract target user ID.'); }

item.name: PUT /rbac/owner/assign → 201 (grant owner role to acting user)

exec: if (!token) { console.error('[ERROR] up_access_token not set — setup/POST /login did not complete.'); }
if (!secret) { console.error('[ERROR] bootstrap_secret not set in environment — add it to environment.json.'); }
console.log('[INFO] Acting user is now owner — RBAC.Require(rbac:read) and RBAC.Require(rbac:grant_user_perm) will pass on subsequent requests.');
const id = pm.collectionVariables.get('up_target_user_id'); if (!id) { console.error('[ERROR] up_target_user_id not set.'); }
pm.test('permissions list is empty (no grants yet)', () => { pm.expect(body.permissions).to.have.lengthOf(0); });
if (!permID) { console.error('[ERROR] up_perm_id not set — GET /rbac/permissions step did not complete.'); }
pm.test('scope is own (default when not specified)', () => { pm.expect(body.scope).to.eql('own'); });
pm.test('granted_at is a parseable date', () => { pm.expect(isNaN(new Date(body.granted_at).getTime())).to.be.false; });
pm.test('expires_at is a parseable date in the future', () => { const d = new Date(body.expires_at); pm.expect(isNaN(d.getTime())).to.be.false; pm.expect(d.getTime()).to.be.above(Date.now()); });
pm.test('permissions list has exactly one entry', () => { pm.expect(body.permissions).to.be.an('array').and.have.lengthOf(1); });

pm.test('permission entry has required fields', () => { const p = body.permissions[0]; pm.expect(p.id).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(p.canonical_name).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(p.name).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(p.resource_type).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(p.scope).to.be.a('string').and.not.empty; pm.expect(p.granted_reason).to.eql('E2E test grant'); pm.expect(isNaN(new Date(p.granted_at).getTime())).to.be.false; pm.expect(isNaN(new Date(p.expires_at).getTime())).to.be.false; });
const grantID = pm.collectionVariables.get('up_grant_id'); if (!grantID) { console.error('[ERROR] up_grant_id not set — POST /permissions step did not complete.'); }
pm.test('permissions list is empty after revoke', () => { pm.expect(body.permissions).to.be.an('array').and.have.lengthOf(0); });

item.name: GET /rbac/users/{unknown_user_id}/permissions — user has no grants → 200 empty
POST — expires_at in the past → 422 expires_at_in_past

exec: const pad = 'x'.repeat(1024 * 1024 + 1); pm.request.body.update({ mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ permission_id: 'any', granted_reason: 'test', expires_at: '2099-01-01T00:00:00Z', _pad: pad }) });

item.name: POST — grant permission (setup for conflict test)

exec: if (!permID) { console.error('[ERROR] up_perm_id not set — happy-path/GET /rbac/permissions did not complete.'); }
console.log('[INFO] Conflict setup grant created:', pm.response.json().id);
const grantID = pm.collectionVariables.get('up_conflict_grant_id'); if (!grantID) { console.error('[ERROR] up_conflict_grant_id not set — conflict setup grant did not capture the ID.'); }
pm.test('status is 204 (conflict grant revoked, DB clean)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(204));
